brand_dna.category: Ultra-premium mechanical toolwatches engineered for spaceflight - a watchmaking brand playing in the aerospace-grade, collector frontier lane.
brand_dna.core_belief: We use emerging technologies and ideas to build products for the next chapter of space exploration - built on curiosity, optimism, and our capacity as humans to shape a positive future.
proof_points: Monolith developed over 6 years, tested against Apollo-era EVA/IVA protocols and re-developed through modern engineering.
Materials and manufacturing processes meet ISO aerospace standards and NASA material guidance.
Aircore chassis 3D printed in Scalmalloy - strength of titanium at nearly half the weight, 6.5g chassis.
Founder Karel Bachand did research at NASA Ames; Project One featured by Forbes and semi-finalist for the Louis Vuitton Watch Prize.
brand_dna.positioning: Top-tier, technical and mission-driven - not a heritage luxury house but an engineering-first independent building tools for life beyond Earth.

voice_pillars.name: Frontier-Minded
voice_pillars.desc: Writes with optimistic, mission-scale ambition that frames the product as part of a larger human story.
voice_pillars.quote: Tools for the next chapter of space exploration

voice_pillars.name: Engineering-First
voice_pillars.desc: Leads with hard specs, materials, and processes rather than emotion or lifestyle fluff.
voice_pillars.quote: Strength of titanium at nearly half the weight.

voice_pillars.name: Built to Endure
voice_pillars.desc: Emphasizes permanence and survival in extreme conditions, positioning the object as generational.
voice_pillars.quote: engineered to last for not just generations, but millennia

voice_pillars.name: Poetic-Technical
voice_pillars.desc: Blends precise engineering with a spiritual, almost reverent sense of purpose.
voice_pillars.quote: A mechanical timepiece... bridging technical and spiritual realms.

phrases: your companion for life beyond Earth
built to withstand the harsh environments of space
understandable, serviceable, and adaptable over time

icp: Serious watch collectors and space-and-technology enthusiasts with high disposable income who value engineering innovation, provable performance, and being early to a frontier story.

personas.name: Frontier Collector
personas.description: A high-net-worth independent-watch collector who buys firsts and grails - moved by the 'world's first' engineering feats and limited, mission-proven provenance.

personas.name: Aerospace Technophile
personas.description: An engineer, pilot, or space-industry insider who scrutinizes materials and specs - moved by Scalmalloy, ISO aerospace standards, and Apollo-era EVA/IVA testing.

personas.name: Optimist Futurist
personas.description: A patron of the space renaissance who wants to own a piece of humanity's next chapter - moved by mission, legacy, and the Memory Disc story.

offers: Monolith priced at $9,750 in three strap colorways (Barrelhand Orange, Black, Black & Orange).
Newsletter subscription for exclusive updates and early access to new products.
Downloadable non-commercial Monolith Source Files under the Barrelhand R&D License (BRDL v1.1).

allowed.claim: Materials and manufacturing processes meet ISO aerospace standards and NASA material guidance.
Each component is tested against EVA/IVA protocols established during the Apollo era and further developed through modern engineering.
Aircore chassis built from Scalmalloy via 3D printing - an ultralight 6.5g chassis.
Developed over 6 years from the ground up.
Monolith is fully refundable prior to shipment.
Project One was limited to 10 pieces, designed and manufactured in the USA, and is sold out.

banned.why: Meeting NASA material guidance is not an endorsement or certification; implying agency approval is banned per compliance rules.

banned.claim: The watch you can buy has flown in / been proven in space.
banned.why: Only test units relate to flight; claiming the retail Monolith has flown implies unsupported flight-proven status - banned.

banned.claim: The best space watch in the world / the #1 toolwatch.
banned.why: FTC bans unqualified superlatives that cannot be objectively substantiated.
Aspirational durability language cannot be presented as a guaranteed, provable result under FTC substantiation.

banned.claim: Limited units left - order in the next X hours.
banned.why: Fake or invented urgency is banned; only state real, verifiable batch and delivery timing.

banned.claim: Trusted by astronauts / endorsed by NASA engineers.
banned.why: No such endorsements are stated on the site; invented endorsements are banned under FTC rules.
